ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tasks" (
  "task_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each task */,
  "task_name" TEXT /* Name of the task */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the task */,
  "complexity" TEXT /* Complexity level of the task (easy, medium, hard) */,
  "default_model" TEXT /* Default machine learning model recommended for the task */,
  "performance_metrics" TEXT /* Performance metrics for the task (e.g., accuracy, precision, recall) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("task_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "formats" (
  "format_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each format */,
  "format_name" TEXT /* Name of the format */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the format */,
  "file_extension" TEXT /* File extension associated with the format */,
  "compression_type" TEXT /* Type of compression used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("format_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "creators" (
  "creator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each creator */,
  "creator_name" TEXT /* Full name of the creator */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation of the creator */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the creator */,
  "created_datasets" INTEGER /* Number of datasets created by the creator */,
  "research_interests" TEXT /* Research interests of the creator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("creator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "references" (
  "reference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reference */,
  "reference_link" TEXT /* Link to the reference */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the reference */,
  "citation_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the reference has been cited */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Date of publication of the reference */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reference_id")
)
column_names: Complexity level of the task (easy, medium, hard)
Default machine learning model recommended for the task
Performance metrics for the task (e.g., accuracy, precision, recall)
File extension associated with the format
Number of datasets created by the creator
Number of times the reference has been cited
Date of publication of the reference
